heading: Change of name; Puerto Rico
label: 731a Change of name; Puerto Rico
text: From and after , the island designated “Porto Rico” in the Act entitled “An Act to provide a civil government for Porto Rico, and for other purposes,” approved , as amended, shall be known and designated as “Puerto Rico.” All laws, regulations, and public documents and records of the United States in which such island is designated or referred to under the name of “Porto Rico” shall be held to refer to such island under and by the name of “Puerto Rico.”May 17, 1932March 2, 1917
notes: References in TextAct approved , as amended, referred to in text, is , , known as the Puerto Rican Federal Relations Act and also popularly known as the Jones Act, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.March 2, 1917act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 14539 Stat. 951section 731 of this title
CodificationSection was not enacted as part of the Puerto Rican Federal Relations Act which comprises this chapter.
